title: Antenna structure with hollow-boresight antenna beam
abstract: In accordance with one or more embodiments, a communication device includes a dielectric antenna having a longitudinal axis, a feed point and an aperture. A cable comprising a core is coupled to the feed point of the dielectric antenna. A transmitter, coupled to the cable, facilitates a transmission of first electromagnetic waves to the feed point of the dielectric antenna, the first electromagnetic waves guided by the core. The first electromagnetic waves propagate along the core without requiring an electrical return path, and the first electromagnetic waves generate free-space wireless signals from the aperture of the antenna in accordance with a hollow-boresight antenna beam pattern.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: 1. A communication device, comprising: a dielectric antenna having a longitudinal axis, a feed point and an aperture; a cable comprising a core coupled to the feed point of the dielectric antenna; and a transmitter, coupled to the cable, the transmitter operable to: facilitate a transmission of first electromagnetic waves to the feed point of the dielectric antenna, the first electromagnetic waves having a TMOO wave mode, the first electromagnetic waves guided by the core, the first electromagnetic waves propagating along the core without requiring an electrical return path, and the first electromagnetic waves generating free-space wireless signals from the aperture of the dielectric antenna in accordance with a hollow-boresight antenna beam pattern, wherein the hollow- boresight antenna beam pattern has a null in a direction corresponding to the longitudinal axis of the dielectric antenna; facilitate a transmission of second electromagnetic waves to the feed point of the dielectric antenna, the second electromagnetic waves having a HE11 wave mode, the second electromagnetic waves guided by the core, the second electromagnetic waves propagating along the core without requiring an electrical return path, and the second electromagnetic waves generating free-space wireless signals from the aperture of the dielectric antenna in accordance with a boresight antenna beam pattern, wherein the boresight antenna beam pattern has a lobe in the direction corresponding to the longitudinal axis of the dielectric antenna. 2. The communication device of claim 1, wherein the dielectric antenna has a circular cross section. 3. The communication device of claim 1, wherein the dielectric antenna is symmetrical about the longitudinal axis of the dielectric antenna. 4. The communication device of claim 1, wherein the hollow-boresight antenna beam pattern is symmetrical about the longitudinal axis of the dielectric antenna. 5. The communication device of claim 1, wherein dielectric antenna is conductorless. 6. The communication device of claim 1, wherein the transmitter generates electromagnetic signals that convey data at non-optical frequencies and wherein the communication device further comprises a coupler configured to receive the electromagnetic signals and further to launch the first electromagnetic waves and the second electromagnetic waves on the core. 7. The communication device of claim 1, wherein the dielectric antenna has a circular conical cross section. 8. The communication device of claim 7, wherein the dielectric antenna is symmetrical about the longitudinal axis of the dielectric antenna. 9. The communication device of claim 8, the hollow-boresight antenna beam pattern azimuthally symmetrical about the longitudinal axis of the dielectric antenna. 10. A method, comprising: providing a dielectric antenna having a longitudinal axis, a feed point and an aperture; providing a cable comprising a core coupled to the feed point of the dielectric antenna; and providing a transmitter, coupled to the cable, the transmitter operable to: facilitate a transmission of first electromagnetic waves to the feed point of the dielectric antenna, the first electromagnetic waves having a TM00 wave mode, the first electromagnetic waves guided by the core, the first electromagnetic waves propagating along the core without requiring an electrical return path, and the first electromagnetic waves generating free-space wireless signals from the aperture of the dielectric antenna in accordance with a hollow-boresight antenna beam pattern, wherein the hollow- boresight antenna beam pattern has a null in a direction corresponding to the longitudinal axis of the dielectric antenna; facilitate a transmission of second electromagnetic waves to the feed point of the dielectric antenna, the second electromagnetic waves having a HE11 wave mode, the second electromagnetic waves guided by the core, the second electromagnetic 

 waves propagating along the core without requiring an electrical return path, and the second electromagnetic waves generating free-space wireless signals from the aperture of the dielectric antenna in accordance with a boresight antenna beam pattern, wherein the boresight antenna beam pattern has a lobe in the direction corresponding to the longitudinal axis of the dielectric antenna. 11. The method of claim 10, wherein dielectric antenna is conductorless. 12. The method of claim 10, wherein the transmitter generates electromagnetic signals that convey data at non-optical frequencies. 13. The method of claim 10, wherein the dielectric antenna has a circular conical cross section. 14. The method of claim 13, wherein the dielectric antenna is symmetrical about the longitudinal axis of the dielectric antenna. 15. The method of claim 14, the hollow-boresight antenna beam pattern azimuthally symmetrical about the longitudinal axis of the dielectric antenna.
